rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSE MARIE TAYLOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Objections to the petition have been filed. 2) Petitioner paid $3,900 to A People's Choice prior to her retaining an attorney and requests reimbursement for that amount. Petitioner has not sufficiently alleged why these are not covered by her statutory fees, as these are services covered by statutory fees. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 8th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 8th amended general order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST AND LAST NAME.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/16/2021 to be heard with the related trust accounting petition (PTR-19-302694). 1) Paragraph 8 of the petition refers to "recording letters and preparing proof of the same" as one of the services rendered by the petitioner's attorneys, but no proof of recording letters is on file. 2) Schedules E & F do not show CUSIP numbers of Newmont and Weyerhaeuser stocks. 3) Schedule F shows the number of shares of Weyerhaeuser different from the number of shares reported on Schedule E. 4) Schedule H does not explain the Weyerhaeuser stock split. 5) Pages 6-7 of Exhibit B are missing from two sets of courtesy copies. Please provide a complete copy of Exhibit B. 6) Please identify "items of value" retrieved from the storage and described in the petitioner's time entry on 08/31/2019 (Exhibit B). Have those items been inventoried and appraised? 7) Please explain why the petitioner approved the engagement and fees of the care agency in which the trustee of the conservatee's trust has a material financial interest.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Modify Trust, To Compel Co-Trustees To Account, To Remove Co-Trustees, To Declare Deeds Void; For Redress Of Breaches Of Trust; To Enjoin
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/17/2021. Issues: 1) Please file a joint status statement to update the court on the current status of the case and the remaining issues. 2) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. 3) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7].

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the purpose of admitting the will dated May 18, 2006. No appearance is required. Status of closing the estate will be set on 8/17/2022. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least one week before 8/17/2022. Appearance will be required on 8/17/2022 if no petition has been filed. (It is noted, however, that any pleading by a Court-appointed fiduciary or a proposed fiduciary must be made by that fiduciary or proposed fiduciary, not by the attorney on the attorney's information and belief. Also, when submitting courtey copies to the Court, they should not be submitted as one mass of copies clipped together as one document.)

Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.]

rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required to discuss the need to open a probate administration in light of the value of the property and probate code section . Mr. Spencer must appear. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/17/2021. Issues: 1) The Petitioner's phone number is required on the Duties and Liabilities form. Please file a Duties and Liabilities form with all the required information. 2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. 3) Petitioner has not filed Proof of Publication. Note: Filing Declaration of Real Property form SFA-001 is no longer a requirement. See the updated local rules. https://www.sfsuperiorcourt.org/general-info/local-rules
